terms.label: World facts
terms.definition: Store, query, persist, and migrate typed facts that drive interactions and scene conditions.

terms.label: Map layers
terms.definition: Enable or replace visual, collision, and interaction layers from explicit world state.

terms.label: Route gates
terms.definition: Open, close, and validate routes while guaranteeing a documented recovery path.

terms.label: Inventory
terms.definition: Grant, inspect, consume, and persist abstract items or capabilities with readable feedback.

terms.label: Context interaction
terms.definition: Resolve a player interaction against nearby actors, props, conditions, and available verbs.

terms.label: NPC state
terms.definition: Persist character availability, schedule, belief, tactic, and dialogue-gate state.

terms.label: Dialogue gates
terms.definition: Select dialogue or performance beats from player knowledge and reveal-safe world conditions.

terms.label: Performance cues
terms.definition: Sequence actor motion, animation, audio, lighting, text, and interaction windows deterministically.

terms.label: Camera cues
terms.definition: Pan, frame, focus, shake, or transition the camera while preserving navigation readability.

terms.label: Portal transitions
terms.definition: Move the player between map regions with explicit spawn, facing, and state preservation.

terms.label: Grid collision
terms.definition: Represent walkability and dynamic blockers on a deterministic top-down tile or cell grid.

terms.label: Spatial query
terms.definition: Find nearby actors, props, zones, and line-of-sight relations for interactions and feedback.

terms.label: Object placement
terms.definition: Place or move bounded objects into legal cells and derive interactions from their positions.

terms.label: Object composition
terms.definition: Combine bounded properties or object roles under deterministic and inspectable composition rules.

terms.label: Schedule clock
terms.definition: Advance, display, persist, and reset a finite schedule without depending on real wall-clock time.

terms.label: Checkpoints
terms.definition: Capture and restore safe player, map, inventory, NPC, and reveal state consistently.

terms.label: Save migration
terms.definition: Version, validate, and migrate saved state without silently discarding authoritative facts.

terms.label: Hint ladder
terms.definition: Offer escalating optional hints tied to failed hypotheses while avoiding premature revelation.

terms.label: Route simulation
terms.definition: Explore required state and topology routes automatically to detect unreachable goals and softlocks.

terms.label: Experience telemetry
terms.definition: Record privacy-safe actions, attempts, revisits, hints, and comprehension proxies for evaluation.

governance.changePolicy: Capabilities require an observable contract, fallback policy, and adapter/runtime verification fixture.
